2026-04-21T09:04:44.6090257Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T09:04:44.6090634Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T09:04:44.6090955Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

I need to clarify whether the "Auto Fix" column indicates what's currently implemented versus what should be attempted, or if I'm misinterpreting the instructions entirely. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Review comment content in Markdown. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line.","type":["number","string"]},"path":{"description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js'). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. 2026-04-21T09:09:04.9269081Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m

2026-04-21T09:09:04.9269771Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
